CKSPEC-AGENT-001.violation_evidence: The `test -f AGENTS.md` check is run by `just conform` (CI conform job); a missing AGENTS.md fails the check and conform exits non-zero.

CKSPEC-AGENT-002.violation_evidence: Analysis: AGENTS.md carries no Claude/Gemini/Copilot-specific instructions (provider rules live in CLAUDE.md). A grep can't distinguish a provider *reference* (e.g. 'see CLAUDE.md') from a provider *instruction*, so the absence is verified at review. Artifact: AGENTS.md.

CKSPEC-AGENT-003.violation_evidence: The `test -f CLAUDE.md` check is run by `just conform` (CI conform job); a missing CLAUDE.md fails the check and conform exits non-zero.

CKSPEC-AGENT-004.evidence: AGENTS.md covers: purpose, architecture, commands, conventions, testing, troubleshooting.
CKSPEC-AGENT-004.violation_evidence: Analysis: AGENTS.md covers purpose, architecture, commands, conventions, testing, and troubleshooting. 'Sufficient for common tasks without other docs' is a sufficiency judgment a check can't make; verified at review. Artifact: AGENTS.md section structure.

CKSPEC-AGENT-005.evidence: --output json flag provides machine-readable output. No protocol layer required.
CKSPEC-AGENT-005.violation_evidence: crates/cli/tests/cli.rs JSON-mode tests exercise the --output json agent interface end to end; a regression fails them in CI (`just check`).

CKSPEC-AGENT-006.evidence: A `catalog` command emits the cross-impl catalog schema via the OUT-002 envelope, derived from the clap command tree (Cli::command()). Schema types in the framework crate (ckeletin::catalog, re-exported via infrastructure); clap walk in crates/cli/src/catalog.rs.
CKSPEC-AGENT-006.violation_evidence: The catalog is derived from the SAME clap Command tree the parser uses, so it cannot drift from the command set (anti-drift is structural). crates/cli/tests/cli.rs catalog_* tests assert the emitted catalog is a valid OUT-002 success envelope that lists every subcommand (incl. catalog itself) with global flags carrying takes_value; framework unit tests in .ckeletin/crate/src/catalog.rs cover serialization. A regression fails them in CI (`just check`).

CKSPEC-ARCH-001.evidence: Cargo workspace members: crates/domain, crates/infrastructure, crates/cli, plus framework crates .ckeletin/crate and .ckeletin/conform
CKSPEC-ARCH-001.violation_evidence: Structural: crates/domain/Cargo.toml has no dep on infrastructure/cli. crates/infrastructure/Cargo.toml has no dep on domain/cli. Import violations tested via trybuild: crates/domain/tests/violations/domain_imports_infrastructure.rs

CKSPEC-ARCH-002.evidence: Domain Cargo.toml has no infrastructure/cli deps. Infra has no domain/cli deps.

CKSPEC-ARCH-003.evidence: Only crates/cli/Cargo.toml lists clap. Domain and infrastructure cannot import it.

CKSPEC-ARCH-004.evidence: Domain crate depends only on serde. No cross-domain, infra, or framework imports.

CKSPEC-ARCH-005.evidence: Infrastructure Cargo.toml has no domain or cli dependency.

CKSPEC-ARCH-006.evidence: crates/cli/src/main.rs is the bootstrap entry — arg parsing, config loading, logging init, and dispatch. Feature logic lives in domain; the entry contains no business logic.
CKSPEC-ARCH-006.violation_evidence: Structural: only crates/cli/Cargo.toml has [[bin]] target. Domain and infrastructure crates are library-only. Entry-point minimality is a structural/design property — the compiler enforces that only cli has a bin target, but cannot enforce that main.rs stays minimal (that's a code-review / coverage judgment). Hence enforcement_level=design, not compile-time.

CKSPEC-ARCH-007.evidence: All source lives in designated crate directories; no source files at the workspace root. Enforced structurally by the Cargo workspace layout (a stray root .rs file belongs to no crate and is not built) rather than by a dedicated file-placement linter — hence enforcement_level=design, not compile-time.
CKSPEC-ARCH-007.violation_evidence: Structural: crates/cli/Cargo.toml has the only [[bin]] target. crates/domain and crates/infrastructure are library-only. No source files at workspace root.

CKSPEC-CL-001.violation_evidence: The `test -f CHANGELOG.md` check is run by `just conform` (CI conform job); a missing CHANGELOG.md fails the check and conform exits non-zero.

CKSPEC-CL-002.evidence: CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog format.
CKSPEC-CL-002.violation_evidence: Analysis: CHANGELOG.md uses Keep a Changelog categories (Added/Changed/Deprecated/Removed/Fixed/Security), reverse-chronological order, and version+date headings. Strict format conformance is review-verified — no structural linter is wired. Artifact: CHANGELOG.md.

CKSPEC-CL-003.violation_evidence: The check requires an ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) dated version heading in CHANGELOG.md; a regional date format would not match.

CKSPEC-CL-004.evidence: Cargo.toml version field. CHANGELOG states SemVer adherence.
CKSPEC-CL-004.violation_evidence: The check requires the SemVer-adherence statement in CHANGELOG.md; the version lives in Cargo.toml (workspace.package.version).

CKSPEC-CL-005.violation_evidence: The check fails if the [Unreleased] section is removed from CHANGELOG.md.

CKSPEC-CL-006.violation_evidence: Analysis: entries are hand-written prose describing features (not a git-log dump) — an editorial property no check can confirm. Artifact: CHANGELOG.md entries.

CKSPEC-CL-007.evidence: Reference-style links at bottom of CHANGELOG.md.
CKSPEC-CL-007.violation_evidence: The check requires a reference-style version-comparison link (…/compare/v…) in CHANGELOG.md.

CKSPEC-ENF-001.evidence: lefthook pre-commit (fmt, clippy), cargo-deny, CI via just check
CKSPEC-ENF-001.violation_evidence: lefthook.yml pre-commit hooks (fmt, clippy, conventional commits) and .github/workflows/ci.yml gating `just check` are tooling that wraps the codebase from outside; a violating commit is rejected pre-commit or fails CI.

CKSPEC-ENF-002.evidence: 6 compile-time, 5 pre-commit/CI, 3 honor-system. See enforcement audit table.
CKSPEC-ENF-002.violation_evidence: Analysis: the ladder (compile-time > linter > script > CI > honor) is realized across the repo and documented in CONFORMANCE.md's enforcement audit table (which mechanism enforces which rule). Whether the strongest feasible mechanism was chosen per rule is a design judgment, not a runtime check. Artifact: CONFORMANCE.md 'Enforcement Audit Table'.

CKSPEC-ENF-003.evidence: CONFORMANCE.md enforcement audit table documents all gaps.
CKSPEC-ENF-003.violation_evidence: Analysis: CONFORMANCE.md's audit table + the documented ENF-007 feedback signals enumerate every honor-system/partial gap and why. Whether a gap was *omitted* cannot be machine-checked (you'd need to know the gap exists), so completeness is review-verified. Artifact: CONFORMANCE.md.

CKSPEC-ENF-004.violation_evidence: Analysis: CONFORMANCE.md maintains the living audit table (decision -> mechanism -> level -> status -> gap), reconciled with conformance-mapping.toml. Table completeness is a review judgment no check can make. Artifact: CONFORMANCE.md 'Enforcement Audit Table' section.

CKSPEC-ENF-005.evidence: The conformance generator (.ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs, run by `just conform`) is hermetic by default: it reads ONLY the committed conformance/requirements.json snapshot (no network, no file writes). `--refresh` (`just conform-refresh`) fetches the latest spec from upstream and rewrites the snapshot for deliberate, reviewed reconciliation. The generator exits non-zero on any unmapped requirement AND on any mapping entry whose ID is not in the spec (stale/invented IDs inflate totals). Both directions are checked before reporting.
CKSPEC-ENF-005.violation_evidence: Unit tests find_unmapped_flags_a_requirement_missing_from_the_mapping and find_extra_flags_a_mapping_entry_not_in_the_spec in .ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs prove both completeness directions; conform exits non-zero (CI conform job).

CKSPEC-ENF-006.evidence: 7 trybuild violation tests back the compile-time claims. `just conform` verifies each declared violation_test file exists and emits an ENF-007 feedback signal for any above-honor-system claim lacking a violation test or violation_evidence. Dangling anchor validation also verifies that evidence file paths and symbol names cited in the mapping actually exist.
CKSPEC-ENF-006.violation_evidence: Unit tests lacks_proof_* and dangling_anchor_* in .ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs prove the proof-detection logic flags above-honor-system claims lacking a violation test or evidence, and that dangling file anchors are caught.

CKSPEC-ENF-007.evidence: `just conform` (.ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs) emits feedback signals for missing violation tests and above-honor-system claims lacking proof; surfaced in the report summary (feedback_signals).
CKSPEC-ENF-007.violation_evidence: The feedback vector and ENF-007 summary in .ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs produce the signals; the CI conform job surfaces them on every run.

CKSPEC-ENF-008.evidence: `just conform` FAILS (exit non-zero) on any `met` requirement that has neither an automated check / violation test NOR written violation_evidence — an unanchored `met` cannot pass the gate.
CKSPEC-ENF-008.violation_evidence: Unit tests anchored_met_passes / unanchored_met_is_rejected in .ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs prove an unanchored `met` is flagged; the anchoring gate runs in the CI conform job.

CKSPEC-ENF-009.evidence: The tag-triggered release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) gates its publish step on the conform job (`needs: conform`), so no release proceeds while conformance fails. A tag-version reconciliation step asserts the pushed tag (vX.Y.Z) equals the cli package CARGO_PKG_VERSION — preventing the historical incident where v0.2.0 was cut from package version 0.1.0. A scheduled spec-drift job continuously checks the live spec_version against the vendored one and opens an issue when upstream advances.
CKSPEC-ENF-009.violation_evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml gates release on the conform job; the tag-version step fails the release when the tag and package version disagree; .github/workflows/ci.yml runs `just conform`; the spec-drift detector job surfaces upstream advances so drift is caught between releases.

CKSPEC-ENF-010.evidence: A deterministic conformance-report.json (sorted keys, no timestamp) is published at the repo root, projected from conformance-mapping.toml. `just conform` regenerates it in-memory and FAILS if the committed report drifted from the mapping (sync-check). The spec repo aggregates this report instead of hand-authoring.
CKSPEC-ENF-010.violation_evidence: Unit tests report_projection_is_deterministic / sync_check_detects_drift in .ckeletin/conform/src/main.rs prove the report is a stable projection and that drift fails the gate; conformance-report.json is the published artifact.

CKSPEC-OUT-001.evidence: output.rs: stdout (data), stderr (tracing status), log file (tracing audit).
CKSPEC-OUT-001.violation_evidence: .ckeletin/crate/src/output.rs unit tests assert stream routing (human→writer, json→envelope on stdout, error→stderr); a routing regression fails them in CI (`just check`).

CKSPEC-OUT-002.evidence: --output json flag. Integration tests verify the JSON envelope on stdout with clean stderr.
CKSPEC-OUT-002.violation_evidence: crates/cli/tests/cli.rs ping_json_mode_* and json_mode_error_* tests assert the JSON envelope on stdout and empty stderr; a regression fails them in CI (`just check`).

CKSPEC-OUT-003.evidence: Envelope struct: status, command, data, error. Unit tests verify serialization.
CKSPEC-OUT-003.violation_evidence: .ckeletin/crate/src/output.rs envelope_* unit tests assert the {status, command, data, error} envelope shape and serialization; a regression fails them in CI (`just check`).

CKSPEC-OUT-004.evidence: Output::success/message/error shadow-log the rendered data (not just the command name) to the audit layer, and file audit logging is on by default (Config.log_file_enabled defaults to true; --no-audit or log_file_enabled=false opts out). Active in both human and JSON modes.
CKSPEC-OUT-004.violation_evidence: Shadow-log unit tests success_shadow_logs_the_rendered_data + message_shadow_logs_the_message_text in .ckeletin/crate/src/output.rs; default-on/opt-out integration tests audit_log_written_under_config_home_by_default + no_audit_flag_disables_the_log_file in crates/cli/tests/cli.rs; content test audit_log_content_contains_output_success_event_and_data in crates/cli/tests/cli.rs

CKSPEC-OUT-005.evidence: Domain, infrastructure, and framework crates each declare `[lints.clippy] print_stdout = 'deny'` and `print_stderr = 'deny'` in their Cargo.toml. A `println!` in domain or infrastructure code fails `cargo clippy` (lint-level, not just compile-time). Verified: adding `println!("x")` to domain code produces `error: use of println!` under the print_stdout lint and the compile fails. The cli crate (presentation layer) is exempt — it is the only crate authorized to write to stdout/stderr via the Output struct.
CKSPEC-OUT-005.violation_evidence: crates/domain/Cargo.toml, crates/infrastructure/Cargo.toml, and .ckeletin/crate/Cargo.toml each carry `[lints.clippy] print_stdout = 'deny' / print_stderr = 'deny'`. These lints run in `just ckeletin-clippy` (part of `just check`, gated by CI). A regression (adding a println! to domain) fails the lint and hence fails CI. Verified during this conformance wave: a temporary println! probe appended to domain code produced `error: use of println!` (clippy::print_stdout) and nonzero clippy exit; the probe was removed before committing.

CKSPEC-OUT-006.evidence: The `version` command and `--version` surface semver (CARGO_PKG_VERSION), source commit, build date, and a dirty flag via infrastructure::build_info::BuildInfo; each field degrades to an explicit "unknown" (option_env!) rather than being omitted or fabricated.
CKSPEC-OUT-006.violation_evidence: Integration test version_command_json_has_fields in crates/cli/tests/cli.rs asserts version/commit/date/dirty are all present; build_info + version.rs unit tests cover the unknown-degradation and the dirty-suffix parse.

CKSPEC-TEST-001.evidence: TDD enforced via AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Cannot automate intent.
CKSPEC-TEST-001.violation_evidence: Analysis: TDD is an intent no check can verify post-hoc (a test written after code is indistinguishable from one written before). Mandated in AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md; git history shows tests committed atomically with implementation. Artifact: AGENTS.md 'Testing'/'Workflow' + git history.

CKSPEC-TEST-002.evidence: `just coverage` enforces 85% line coverage via cargo-llvm-cov, gated by the CI coverage job (.github/workflows/ci.yml). The build-time conformance generator (.ckeletin/conform) is an excluded, documented denominator; the rest of the workspace is ~99.8%.
CKSPEC-TEST-002.violation_evidence: `--fail-under-lines 85` in the `just coverage` recipe is a documented cargo-llvm-cov gate that exits non-zero below threshold (tooling-enforced); run by the CI coverage job, so a drop below 85% fails the build.

CKSPEC-TEST-003.evidence: Writer injection in Output. No mock frameworks in Cargo.lock.
CKSPEC-TEST-003.violation_evidence: The check fails if a mock-framework crate enters Cargo.lock; the writer-injection pattern (Output takes &mut dyn Write) is the DI mechanism in .ckeletin/crate/src/output.rs.

CKSPEC-TEST-004.evidence: Enforced via AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Cannot automate grouping.
CKSPEC-TEST-004.violation_evidence: Analysis: test+impl commit grouping is an intent no check can verify after the fact. Mandated in AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md; git history shows atomic test+implementation commits. Artifact: AGENTS.md + git history.
